pipeline.desc: The full hardened content chain (scout -> writer -> grader gate -> video -> reporter). SOLE content entry point per the locked rule — the granular entries below exist for manual runs and experiments, not for scheduling alongside this.

scout.desc: Research collector: mines trending/outlier content across platforms into the research tabs.

maker.desc: Content writer: turns new research signals into on-brand content packages awaiting review (emails an approval request).

grader.desc: Quality gate over pending packages: grades vs the brand-voice spec, archives anything below threshold after one rewrite. As a pipeline sub-run this also sends the daily-brief email at the end.

publisher-queue.desc: Approval poller: reads the operator's email replies; ONLY on an explicit approval does it invoke the scheduler. This is the manual-approval flow — the one path that can publish.

analyst.desc: Weekly analyst: what posted, what performed, attributed conversions, subscriber delta, exactly one recommended adjustment. Also scores experiments and drafts spawn manifests on threshold signals.

brand-voice.desc: Weekly brand-voice maintainer: keeps the voice spec fresh and scans recent output for drift.

librarian.desc: Periodic: inventories the source material and proposes the next digital product (outline + audience + price) to the approval queue. Never publishes.

producer.desc: On approval: drafts the full product from source material in the brand voice, through the grader. Output to the approval queue. Never publishes.

formatter.desc: Packages an approved draft into a clean PDF + a storefront listing draft (title/description/price) to the approval queue. Never publishes.
